message:
fix(core): always reload memdb from pager on attached-schema delegated reads, not just when memdb_rows_loaded is false

Companion to the recent `fix(core): drop materialized-CTE
plumbing on the attached-only branch of
execute_select_via_memdb_fallback` commit.  That commit fixed the
CTE-shadowing trap on the rewrite side; this one fixes the
read-side cache-staleness trap.

When a parent connection delegates an attached-schema read into
the attached connection via `with_attached_connection(...)`,
the attached connection's memdb may already be marked
`memdb_rows_loaded == true` from a prior delegation cycle.  In
between, the parent connection issued writes that committed to
the attached connection's pager.  The attached connection's
memdb still has the pre-write rows, so the stripped query
(e.g. `SELECT SUM(...) FROM t` rewritten from
`SELECT SUM(...) FROM aux.t`) returns the stale aggregate.

Fix: when `refresh_memdb_from_cached_write_txn_if_stale` does
not signal a refresh-needed AND `memdb_rows_loaded == true`,
force a `reload_memdb_from_pager(&op_cx)` anyway.  This is
slightly more expensive than the prior cache-hit path, but
correctness wins: the attached-only branch is the only place
where parent-issued writes can commit to our pager without
going through our own write path, so this is the only place
where the cached-rows-loaded shortcut produces stale reads.

Comment block on the new branch documents the T1.5 invariant
("attached-only delegation reads must observe parent-issued
committed writes") so a future refactor that strips the reload
must first re-derive the proof that no parent can write to our
pager between our last load and this read.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
